entries.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
entries.snippet: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

entries.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
entries.snippet: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

entries.title: Thousands celebrate open-air Mass with Pope Leo in Cameroon - in pictures
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV is on his third day in Cameroon before he heads to Angola on Saturday.

entries.title: White House and Anthropic hold 'productive' meeting amid fears over Mythos model
entries.snippet: The discussion is a sign the AI firm's technology may be too critical for even the US government to do without.

entries.title: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
entries.snippet: President Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

entries.title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
entries.snippet: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

entries.title: Japan reveals new name for 40C-and-hotter days after blistering summer
entries.snippet: The term - kokushobi - translates to "cruelly hot", "brutally hot" or "severely hot", and comes after Japan's hottest summer on record.

entries.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
entries.snippet: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

entries.title: Kanye West concert in Poland cancelled over antisemitic comments
entries.snippet: The UK government blocked the rapper from entering the country over his history of making pro-Nazi and racist statements.

entries.title: 'Mum is finally free!' Pensioner detained by ICE in US returns to France
entries.snippet: Marie-Thérèse had moved to the US after reuniting with her long-lost love, an American man who died in January.

entries.title: Suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
entries.snippet: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

entries.title: Palestinians hand over suspect in 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant in Paris
entries.snippet: Hicham Harb is suspected of directing the attack in the Rue des Rosiers and acting as one of the gunmen who shot at diners.

entries.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
entries.snippet: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

entries.title: Runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search in South Korea
entries.snippet: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

entries.title: Iranian footballers say Australia has given them 'hope' for safe future
entries.snippet: The duo had sought aslyum after their football team did not sing the national anthem during a match.

entries.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
entries.snippet: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

entries.title: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
entries.snippet: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

entries.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices in London
entries.snippet: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man appear in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

entries.title: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
entries.snippet: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

entries.title: Rescuers to use air cushions in latest effort to save stranded whale
entries.snippet: "Timmy" has been stranded in the Baltic Sea for weeks despite several attempts to free the ailing animal.

entries.title: Opinion: Remembering Kevin Klose, former NPR president and broadcasting icon
entries.snippet: We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.

entries.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
entries.snippet: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

entries.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
entries.snippet: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

entries.title: She invited her friends to come together to make her casket
entries.snippet: Friends gathered at a weaver's studio in Massachusetts to help MaddyChristine Hope Brokopp make her casket.

entries.title: Caracas' iconic macaws threatened by vanishing palm trees
entries.snippet: In the Venezuelan capital, Caracas, flocks of colorful macaws that once brightened city skies now face disappearing nest sites — and with them, a unique urban bond.

entries.title: Uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz remains, as ceasefire nears its end
entries.snippet: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.

entries.title: What's it like to negotiate with Iran? We asked people who have done it
entries.snippet: Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.

entries.title: Not just Trump: Redistricting race will hinge on what these state leaders did
entries.snippet: An 82-year-old Virginia senator raising the stakes, an Indiana consensus builder and a Texas enforcer are among state officials who have shaped the course of the midterm redistricting race.

entries.title: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed
entries.snippet: Jay found himself sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, after attempting to break into a building on his college campus. A fellow inmate's unexpected words brought him comfort — and changed the course of his life.

entries.title: Judge halts local TV giant Nexstar's takeover of rival Tegna until trial
entries.snippet: U.S. Judge Trevor Nunley ruled that consumers could suffer irreparable harm if Nexstar integrated Tegna's stations into its own operations ahead of an antitrust trial.

entries.title: After weeks of fighting, ceasefire sparks cautious celebration in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Iran declared that it would reopen the Strait of Hormuz to commercial sea traffic, which President Trump eagerly supported in a social media post. But Trump also declared that the U.S. blockade of Iran's ports could continue. Meanwhile, the day-old truce between Israel and Hezbol

entries.title: Timeline for Strait of Hormuz shipping recovery remains uncertain
entries.snippet: To discuss the status of the Strait of Hormuz and if it's really open to all ships, Amna Nawaz spoke with Ian Ralby. He is president of Auxilium Worldwide, a non-profit organization that focuses on ocean governance and maritime law and security.

entries.title: News Wrap: Senate approves short-term renewal of FISA surveillance program
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Friday, the Senate approved a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, millions across the Midwest are facing the risk of severe storms and French officials say an 85-year-old widow of an American military veteran has

entries.title: Federal judge dismisses DOJ lawsuit seeking personal details about Rhode Island voters
entries.snippet: U.S. District Court Judge Mary McElroy sided with Rhode Island's top election officials and civil rights advocates, writing that federal law does not permit the U.S. Department of Justice "to conduct the kind of fishing expedition it seeks here."

entries.title: How Trump's recent actions are affecting his support among young voters
entries.snippet: President Trump is on the road this week, speaking at a Turning Point USA rally Friday night in Phoenix. White House correspondent Liz Landers on how the administration's recent actions are affecting his support among young voters.

entries.title: Judge sides with Arizona election official in ruling that could affect midterms voting
entries.snippet: The top election official in Arizona's most populous county will get more authority in running elections after a judge sided with his office in a prolonged legal fight with the local board that shares responsibility for overseeing the vote.

entries.title: One in three Americans forced to make financial sacrifices for health coverage
entries.snippet: At a moment when there's a lot of political attention around questions of affordability, rising health care costs are a growing concern for many Americans. That's especially true for those who are worried about being able to afford insurance premiums after an expansion of subsidi

entries.title: Ex-Kennedy Center staffer alleges chaos and cronyism under Trump leadership
entries.snippet: Unless courts intervene, the Kennedy Center will shut down this July for two years, as part of a roughly $250 million renovation. In the lead-up, there's been a wave of layoffs and a controversial rebranding by President Trump's allies. Josef Palermo was among those laid off and

entries.title: Brooks and Capehart on Trump and Vance clashing with Pope Leo
entries.snippet: David Brooks of The Atlantic and Jonathan Capehart of MS NOW join Amna Nawaz to discuss the week in politics, including President Trump claiming Iran has "agreed to everything" in talks with the U.S. and is suspending its nuclear program, Trump publicly feuding with Pope Leo and

entries.title: Coast Guard searches for 6 after losing contact with boat disabled by Typhoon Sinlaku
entries.snippet: Super Typhoon Sinlaku began battering the Northern Mariana Islands earlier this week, causing damage on the islands of Tinian and Saipan and flash flooding in Guam, the site of several American military bases.

entries.title: White House chief of staff to meet with Anthropic CEO over its new Mythos AI model
entries.snippet: The meeting comes after tensions have run hot between the Trump administration and the safety-conscious Anthropic, which has sought to put guardrails on the development of AI to minimize any potential risks and maximize its economic and national security benefits for the U.S.

entries.title: London police investigate discarded items near Israeli Embassy
entries.snippet: The Metropolitan Police said the embassy was not attacked but that its officers, including some in protective clothing, were combing the area around the plush neighborhood of Kensington Gardens.

entries.title: Patients and dental community mourn Dr. Cerina Fairfax, killed by Virginia ex-Lt. Gov. Fairfax
entries.snippet: Patients and colleagues are remembering Dr. Cerina Fairfax as a devoted mother, a caring dentist and the rock at the center of her family, after police say she was killed by her estranged husband, former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump speaks at Turning Point Action event in Arizona
entries.snippet: The president's rare trip out West comes as Trump faces growing political pressure to wrap up the war and focus on a message that helps his party as they try to defend their congressional majorities in November's elections.

entries.title: 85-year-old widow detained by ICE returns home, France's foreign minister says
entries.snippet: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detained Marie-Thérèse Ross in Alabama on April 1 for overstaying her visa.

entries.title: What to know about singer D4vd's arrest in 14-year-old's killing
entries.snippet: Seven months after a 14-year-old girl's body was found in D4vd's towed SUV, the alt-pop singer has been arrested on suspicion of killing her, authorities said.

entries.title: A transgender teen's case in Ecuador opens path for others seeking legal recognition
entries.snippet: The moment marks a significant step in a country where LGBTQ+ rights have largely advanced through court rulings rather than political action.

entries.title: ICE acting director will resign at the end of May, DHS officials say
entries.snippet: Todd Lyons, who was named acting director in March 2025, led the agency at the center of President Donald Trump's plans to reshape immigration to the U.S.

entries.title: Supreme Court gives oil and gas companies win in Louisiana environmental lawsuit
entries.snippet: The 8-0 procedural decision gives the companies a new day in federal court after a state jury ordered Chevron to pay upward of $740 million to clean up damage to the state's coastline, one of multiple similar lawsuits.

entries.title: Former President Win Myint freed in broad Myanmar prisoner amnesty
entries.snippet: State-run media reported the amnesty on Friday, marking the traditional New Year.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
entries.snippet: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

entries.title: Displaced by Iran war: out of Lebanon, into Syrian crisis
entries.snippet: More than 227,000 people have fled conflict in Lebanon for Syria. But there they have found a lack of housing, economic hardship and no real state services. Despite a new ceasefire, many say they will stay in Syria.

entries.title: Germany news: Iran war not causing migration wave — Dobrindt
entries.snippet: German Interior Minister Dobrindt says that no increased migration to Europe has so far occurred amid the Iran war. An explosion in an underpass in a western town has killed one person. DW has more.

entries.title: India: Parliament fails to pass women's quota bill after delimitation uproar
entries.snippet: Indian government's bid to redraw voting boundaries tied to quotas for women legislators was blocked in a parliament vote. It is the first major legislative setback for PM Modi's government. DW has the latest.

entries.title: US extends waiver allowing purchase of Russian oil
entries.snippet: The US has renewed a waiver permitting countries to buy Russian oil for another month. The decision came two days after the Trump administration had said it had no plans to extend the waiver.

entries.title: World Cup 2026: Fans to pay $150 for train ticket to stadium
entries.snippet: Transportation costs for World Cup fans traveling to US stadiums could be more than 10 times higher than regular fares. Local officials and FIFA bosses are trading blame for the increased prices while fans feel fleeced.

entries.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

entries.title: Can the 25th Amendment remove Donald Trump from office?
entries.snippet: After Donald Trump threatened to wipe out "an entire civilization," clashed with the pope and posted an AI image of himself as Jesus, US Democrats and some Republicans are questioning his fitness to serve as president.

entries.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
entries.snippet: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

entries.title: Kanye West concert canceled in Poland
entries.snippet: The Polish government had urged organizers "not to make public space available to promoters of a criminal ideology." Kanye has disavowed and apologized his previous pro-Nazi stance, linking it to his bipolar disorder.

entries.title: India: Modi plan for women's quota in parliament fails vote
entries.snippet: Opposition parties accused the government of using the quota to increase its own influence in the Indian parliament. The proposal would have seen hundreds more seats added and a 33% quota for women.

entries.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
entries.snippet: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

entries.title: Pope Leo XIV to Cameroon's faithful — 'reject violence'
entries.snippet: Some 120,000 cheering Catholics attended Pope Leo XIV's Friday mass in Cameroon. The pontiff implored those gathered to be the architects of peace and to reject the deceit of violence and abuse.

entries.title: Iran: Internet blackout highlights real toll of censorship
entries.snippet: For Iran's government, the internet shutdown is about control. But for millions of ordinary Iranians, it is a daily reminder that access to the wider world, and even running a business, is controlled by the state.

entries.title: Iran war: What does Tehran expect from fresh talks with US?
entries.snippet: Amid talk of a second round of negotiations between Iran and the US, experts say a breakthrough between the two sides is possible, but far from certain.

entries.title: India’s harvest festivals under climate strain
entries.snippet: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

entries.title: Iran war: Strait of Hormuz open amid Israel-Lebanon truce
entries.snippet: As a 10-day ceasefire deal between Lebanon and Israel took effect, the Israeli military said its forces are going to remain in southern Lebanon. Iran and Donald Trump say the Strait of Hormuz is now open.

entries.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
entries.snippet: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

entries.title: UK: 'Furious' PM Starmer refuses to step down over Mandelson
entries.snippet: Pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer is growing over his naming of Peter Mandelson as US ambassador despite red flags. The PM says he was not told that Mandelson failed a background check before the appointment.
